check: LinkedIn Insight Tag installed with conversion actions mapped to real business outcomes
description: The Insight Tag fires site-wide and conversion actions map to genuine B2B outcomes (demo request, qualified lead, signup), not just page visits
why_it_matters: Same principle as `ADS001` applied to LinkedIn — optimization quality is bottlenecked by conversion signal quality regardless of platform

check: Campaign Groups used to organize by business objective (post-Oct-2025 terminology: Campaign Groups → Campaigns → Ad Sets)
description: Account structure uses the current three-tier hierarchy deliberately, organizing Campaign Groups around real business objectives rather than treating the tiers as arbitrary folders
why_it_matters: Getting the hierarchy's organizing logic right (mirroring `ADS004`'s Google Ads guidance) makes budget allocation and performance analysis tractable as the account grows

check: Lead Gen Forms pre-filled fields verified accurate, form length matched to lead quality needs
description: Native Lead Gen Forms use LinkedIn's pre-fill data accurately and ask for only the fields genuinely needed to qualify a lead, balancing conversion rate against lead quality
why_it_matters: Overly long forms hurt conversion rate; overly short forms produce high-volume but low-quality/unqualified leads — this tradeoff should be a deliberate choice tied to sales capacity, not a default

check: ABM (Account-Based Marketing) matched audiences used for high-value target account lists where applicable
description: For B2B accounts with a defined target account list, LinkedIn's Matched Audiences (company list upload) targets those specific accounts directly rather than relying solely on broad firmographic/interest targeting
why_it_matters: ABM-matched targeting is one of LinkedIn's most distinctive B2B-specific capabilities — underusing it in favor of generic firmographic targeting leaves a real precision advantage on the table for accounts with a defined target list

check: Thought Leader Ads used to promote genuine employee/executive content, not treated as a generic ad unit
description: Thought Leader Ads promote real organic posts from employees/executives with genuine engagement, rather than being used as just another ad format for standard promotional creative
why_it_matters: Thought Leader Ads specifically benefit from and are evaluated partly on the authenticity of the underlying organic content — using it for generic promotional creative forfeits the format's distinct advantage

check: Predictive Audiences (lookalike-style expansion from a seed list) tested for scaling beyond a narrow ABM list
description: Where the target account list is narrow and limiting reach, LinkedIn's Predictive Audiences feature is tested as an expansion mechanism rather than accepting a hard reach ceiling
why_it_matters: Predictive Audiences can meaningfully extend reach from a proven seed list while retaining much of its targeting precision — an underused lever for accounts hitting a reach ceiling on pure ABM targeting

check: TikTok Pixel and Events API both implemented with deduplication (mirrors Meta CAPI pattern)
description: Server-side Events API supplements the browser Pixel with deduplication via a shared event identifier, following the same pattern as `ADS021`'s Meta CAPI guidance
why_it_matters: Signal loss from ad blockers and privacy restrictions affects TikTok tracking the same way it affects Meta — server-side tracking is equally necessary here, not a Meta-specific concern

check: Creative built platform-native from the start, not repurposed from other platforms
description: TikTok creative is produced specifically for the platform's native format and pacing (fast cuts, native sound/trends, vertical-first), not a repurposed Meta Reels or YouTube Shorts asset reused as-is
why_it_matters: Platform-native content consistently and measurably outperforms visibly repurposed content on TikTok specifically — this is one of the most emphasized, well-evidenced platform-specific creative principles in paid social

check: Multiple distinct creative concepts tested for retrieval diversity (same principle as Meta's Andromeda-era guidance)
description: Creative variation represents genuinely different concepts/hooks, not minor edits of the same underlying asset, mirroring `ADS024`'s creative-diversity-for-retrieval principle
why_it_matters: TikTok's delivery system similarly benefits from and evaluates creative diversity as a retrieval signal — the same underlying principle as Meta's applies here, just on a different platform

check: Text and key UI elements kept within platform-specific safe zones, avoiding native UI overlap
description: Creative respects TikTok's specific safe-zone guidelines (avoiding areas where native UI elements like captions, usernames, and engagement buttons overlay the video)
why_it_matters: Text or key visual elements placed in areas the native UI covers become invisible/unreadable to real viewers — this is a simple, mechanical spec-compliance check that's still commonly missed

check: Smart+ automated campaigns given accurate creative and catalog inputs, monitored rather than left unattended
description: TikTok's Smart+ automated campaign type is launched with strong creative diversity and accurate product/conversion data, and monitored periodically rather than assumed to require no oversight
why_it_matters: This mirrors `ADS027`'s Advantage+ guidance — heavily automated campaign types are more, not less, sensitive to input quality, and still benefit from periodic human review

check: TikTok Shop product catalog kept in sync with actual inventory (same principle as EC025's Shopify inventory guidance)
description: For accounts running TikTok Shop, the product catalog feeding ads reflects real-time inventory/pricing, not a stale or manually-maintained separate feed
why_it_matters: Stale product data in shoppable ads directly causes overselling and price-mismatch issues, identical in nature to `EC025`'s general e-commerce inventory guidance

check: Google Ads import reviewed and adjusted, not launched unmodified
description: When importing campaigns from Google Ads, keyword match types, bids, and targeting are reviewed and adjusted for Microsoft Advertising's different audience/auction dynamics rather than assumed to perform identically unmodified
why_it_matters: Bing/Microsoft's search audience (older, higher-income skew on average, different device mix) and auction dynamics differ meaningfully from Google's — an unmodified import often needs real tuning to perform to its potential

check: LinkedIn profile targeting (Microsoft-exclusive) used for B2B campaigns where relevant
description: For B2B advertisers, Microsoft Advertising's LinkedIn-profile-data targeting (industry, company, job function) is tested — a targeting capability not available on Google Ads
why_it_matters: This is a genuinely unique Microsoft-exclusive targeting capability for B2B advertisers, owed to Microsoft's ownership of LinkedIn — underused simply because advertisers default to treating Microsoft as 'just Google's smaller sibling'

check: Microsoft's AI-driven ad placements (Copilot integration, in-market audiences) evaluated for the account's audience fit
description: Newer Microsoft ad surfaces integrating with Copilot and AI-assisted search experiences are evaluated for relevance to the account, not ignored by default just because the surface is newer/smaller
why_it_matters: Being an early, deliberate adopter on emerging high-relevance placements can capture efficient early inventory before it becomes competitively priced — worth evaluating even at smaller scale

check: Lower-competition Microsoft inventory evaluated for cost-efficient volume, not dismissed as too small to matter
description: Microsoft Advertising's typically lower CPCs and less competitive auction are evaluated as a genuine incremental-volume opportunity, not dismissed purely because total search volume is smaller than Google's
why_it_matters: For many verticals, Microsoft Advertising offers a meaningfully better cost-per-conversion than Google at the margin, precisely because of lower competition — dismissing it purely on volume grounds skips real incremental efficiency
